description: Add task-level self-healing capabilities to the OpenClawd agent dispatch system with an 8-stage recovery pipeline, error taxonomy, diagnostic agent, strategy ladder, failure memory, and observability for automatic recovery from agent execution failures.

forbiddenPatterns: Direct database queries bypassing dispatch_db.py connection strategy
Modifying existing error handling in agent_runner.py without preserving original behavior
Adding recovery logic to agent_supervisor.py poll loop that blocks dispatch cycle
Creating new LLM providers - use existing provider registry
Automatic task decomposition - only recommend decomposition to operator
componentContext.discoveryNote: This is a pure backend system. Search agent-dispatch/ for existing error handling patterns. Integrate with health/self_healer.py for provider-level healing coordination. Use structured_logging.py for all recovery events.

systems.name: agent_runner
systems.description: Executes agent tasks with tool call loop, result validation, and error handling. Located at agent-dispatch/agent_runner.py

integration_points.run: Main execution entry point - needs error context capture on all failures
integration_points.RunnerError: Exception raised on execution errors with tool_call_log

gotchas: Tool call loop has depth/loop detection but no recovery integration yet
AgentResult validation happens here - errors must be captured structurally
Raw LLM output is not currently stored - needs new dispatch_runs column

systems.name: agent_supervisor
systems.description: Daemon poll loop that claims tasks, dispatches to agents, handles completion. Located at agent-dispatch/agent_supervisor.py

integration_points.handle_dispatch_failure: Current retry logic with flat 3-retry backoff
integration_points.handle_task_completion: Validates results and flows to OpenClawd
integration_points.poll loop: 30-second cycle - recovery must not block this

gotchas: US-052 implements basic retry - recovery pipeline must integrate without breaking existing retry
US-051 recover_partial_completions sweep handles completion sequence failures
Recovery operations must be atomic to prevent duplicate recovery

systems.name: health_system
systems.description: Provider-level health monitoring and 5-tier self-healing. Located at agent-dispatch/health/

integration_points.self_healer.py: 5-tier provider healing (rate limits, format adapt, fallback, degrade, report)
integration_points.health_monitor.py: Canary tests and provider health tracking

gotchas: Provider healing is orthogonal to task recovery - coordinate but don't duplicate
Diagnostic reports exist for providers - task diagnostic agent is new
Provider health checks may trigger during task recovery - integrate status queries

systems.name: dispatch_db
systems.description: Database connection strategy with WAL mode, busy_timeout, reader/writer separation. Located at agent-dispatch/dispatch_db.py
gotchas: All recovery state must use writer_connection for atomic transitions
dispatch_status transitions must use WHERE guards to prevent races
New columns/tables need migrations in migrations.py

systems.name: structured_logging
systems.description: JSON logging to supervisor.jsonl with event types and durations. Located at agent-dispatch/structured_logging.py

integration_points.log_event: Logs structured JSON events with timestamp, event_type, duration_ms
integration_points.supervisor.jsonl: Log file for all supervisor events

gotchas: Recovery events need new event types: recovery.capture, recovery.classify, etc.
Log to supervisor.jsonl, not separate recovery log file

systemContext.discoveryNote: Search agent-dispatch/ for error handling patterns. Check agent_runner.py for RunnerError structure. Review agent_supervisor.py handle_dispatch_failure for retry integration points.

userStories.title: Add recovery state columns to dispatch_runs table
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the dispatch_runs table to store recovery context so that the diagnostic agent and recovery pipeline have full failure history. **Implementation:** Add all 8 columns from spec Section 8.1: raw_output (TEXT nullable), tool_call_log (JSON TEXT nullable), stop_reason (TEXT nullable), error_context (JSON TEXT nullable), recovery_tier (INTEGER nullable), recovery_strategy (TEXT nullable), lease_extensions (INTEGER DEFAULT 0), tool_calls_count_at_last_extension (INTEGER DEFAULT 0) to dispatch_runs table via migration.
acceptanceCriteria: Add raw_output column to dispatch_runs (TEXT, nullable)
Add tool_call_log column to dispatch_runs (JSON stored as TEXT, nullable)
Add stop_reason column to dispatch_runs (TEXT, nullable)
Add error_context column to dispatch_runs (JSON stored as TEXT, nullable)
Add recovery_tier column to dispatch_runs (INTEGER, nullable)
Add recovery_strategy column to dispatch_runs (TEXT, nullable)
Add lease_extensions column to dispatch_runs (INTEGER, DEFAULT 0)
Add tool_calls_count_at_last_extension column to dispatch_runs (INTEGER, DEFAULT 0)
Migration creates all 8 columns with proper types matching spec Section 8.1
Migration is idempotent (checks if columns exist before adding)
Existing dispatch_runs rows remain valid after migration

userStories.title: Add recovering status to dispatch_status check constraint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want dispatch_status to include 'recovering' so that the recovery pipeline can atomically transition failed tasks to recovery state. **Implementation:** Modify dispatch_status CHECK constraint in tasks table to include 'recovering' in the allowed values.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration updates constraint to allow NULL, 'queued', 'dispatched', 'completed', 'failed', 'interrupted', 'dispatch_failed', 'recovering'
Existing tasks remain valid after constraint update

userStories.title: Create failure_memory table for pattern learning
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a failure_memory table to store error-diagnosis-fix triples so that the recovery system learns from past successes. **Implementation:** Create failure_memory table with: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY), task_id (INTEGER), agent_name (TEXT), error_code (TEXT), error_pattern (TEXT nullable), diagnostic_summary (TEXT), resolution_summary (TEXT), success (BOOLEAN), recovery_tier (INTEGER), created_at (TEXT ISO8601), task_domain (TEXT nullable), similarity_key (TEXT generated column for matching).
acceptanceCriteria: Create failure_memory table with specified columns
id is INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT
task_id has foreign key to tasks(id) with ON DELETE CASCADE
Index on (error_code, agent_name, success) for fast similarity queries

userStories.title: Create recovery_events table for observability
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a recovery_events table to track every recovery pipeline stage for debugging and metrics. **Implementation:** Create recovery_events table with: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY), task_id (INTEGER), trace_id (TEXT for correlating events), event_type (TEXT like 'recovery.capture', 'recovery.classify'), stage (TEXT nullable), duration_ms (INTEGER), metadata (JSON TEXT nullable), created_at (TEXT ISO8601).
acceptanceCriteria: Create recovery_events table with specified columns
id is INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT
task_id has foreign key to tasks(id) with ON DELETE CASCADE
trace_id is TEXT for grouping related recovery events
event_type uses dot notation (recovery.capture, recovery.diagnose.start, etc)
Index on (task_id, created_at) for timeline queries

userStories.title: Create error taxonomy lookup table with recovery parameters
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an ERROR_TAXONOMY dict mapping error codes to categories and recovery parameters so that error classification is deterministic. **Implementation:** Create error_classifier.py with ERROR_TAXONOMY dict mapping error codes (RATE_LIMITED, RESULT_SCHEMA_INVALID, etc) to ErrorCategory dataclass with fields: category (str), max_retries (int), requires_diagnosis (bool), requires_compensation (bool), initial_backoff_seconds (int), backoff_multiplier (float), max_backoff_seconds (int).
acceptanceCriteria: Define ErrorCategory dataclass with specified fields
Define ERROR_TAXONOMY dict with all error codes from spec Section 3
Include all Transient Error codes with appropriate retry counts
Include all Execution Error codes with diagnosis requirement
Include all Configuration Error codes with max_retries=0

userStories.title: Implement detect_error_code function for exception to error code mapping
userStories.description: As a developer, I want detect_error_code() to map raw exceptions and contexts to canonical error codes so that classification is consistent. **Implementation:** In error_classifier.py, create detect_error_code(exception, http_status, agent_result, context) function that inspects exception types (anthropic.RateLimitError, requests.Timeout, json.JSONDecodeError), HTTP status codes, AgentResult fields (status='failed', confidence_score < threshold), and returns error code string.
acceptanceCriteria: detect_error_code() handles json.JSONDecodeError on agent output -> RESULT_PARSE_FAILED
detect_error_code() handles tool loop detection -> TOOL_LOOP_DETECTED
Returns string error code from ERROR_TAXONOMY

userStories.title: Implement classify_error function for error code to category lookup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want classify_error() to look up error codes in ERROR_TAXONOMY and return category with parameters so that recovery pipeline knows how to handle each error. **Implementation:** In error_classifier.py, create classify_error(error_code) that looks up error_code in ERROR_TAXONOMY and returns ErrorCategory. If error_code not found, return default ErrorCategory with category='unknown', max_retries=1, requires_diagnosis=True.
acceptanceCriteria: classify_error() looks up error_code in ERROR_TAXONOMY
Returns ErrorCategory dataclass with all fields
Handles unknown error codes with default category
Default category has max_retries=1 and requires_diagnosis=True

userStories.title: Create ErrorContext dataclass for structured error capture
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an ErrorContext dataclass to structure all failure information so that the recovery pipeline has consistent input. **Implementation:** In recovery/error_capture.py, define ErrorContext dataclass with fields from spec Appendix C.7: error_code, error_category, error_message, error_pattern, task_id, agent_name, task_domain, raw_output, tool_call_log, stop_reason, tokens_used, provider, model, duration_ms, captured_at.
acceptanceCriteria: Define ErrorContext dataclass with all specified fields
error_pattern is Optional[str] for similarity matching
All fields match spec Appendix C.7

userStories.title: Implement capture_error function to extract failure context
userStories.description: As a developer, I want capture_error() to extract all failure information from RunnerError and task context so that diagnostic agent has complete evidence. **Implementation:** In error_capture.py, create capture_error(runner_error, task_row, dispatch_run_row, config) that builds ErrorContext by reading exception, agent output, tool calls, and task metadata. Calls detect_error_code() and classify_error() to populate error_code and error_category.
acceptanceCriteria: capture_error() accepts RunnerError, task dict, dispatch_run dict, config object
Extracts raw agent output from runner_error (if available)
Calls classify_error() with detected error code

userStories.title: Create DiagnosticInput and DiagnosticOutput dataclasses
userStories.description: As a developer, I want structured schemas for diagnostic agent input/output so that diagnostic calls are type-safe. **Implementation:** In recovery/diagnostic_agent.py, define DiagnosticInput and DiagnosticOutput dataclasses matching spec Appendix C.3.
acceptanceCriteria: Define DiagnosticInput dataclass with: task_description, task_domain, agent_name, error_code, error_message, raw_output_excerpt, tool_call_summary, previous_attempts, similar_past_fixes

Define DiagnosticOutput dataclass with: root_cause, root_cause_category, confidence, recommended_strategy, specific_fix, tools_to_adjust, avoid_approaches, needs_human, human_action_needed, is_repeat_failure, cycle_detected
All fields match spec Appendix C.3

userStories.title: Create diagnostic system prompt template
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a diagnostic_sop.md prompt template so that the diagnostic agent analyzes failures consistently. **Implementation:** Create agent-dispatch/prompts/diagnostic_sop.md with analytical prompt following spec Section 5.3 guidelines: focus on root cause, evidence-based reasoning, no fixing (diagnosis only), detect cycles, categorize root cause, recommend strategy.
acceptanceCriteria: Prompt instructs LLM to diagnose, not fix
Requests root cause analysis with evidence
Asks for recommended strategy from ladder
Asks for specific_fix instructions for agent
Prompt is under 1500 tokens (excluding injected context)

userStories.title: Implement diagnose_failure function with LLM call
userStories.description: As a developer, I want diagnose_failure() to make a lightweight LLM call for non-transient errors so that recovery has targeted fix guidance. **Implementation:** In diagnostic_agent.py, create diagnose_failure(diagnostic_input, config) that loads diagnostic_sop.md, injects context, calls config.recovery.diagnostic_model (default claude-haiku-4-5), parses DiagnosticOutput from response, handles token overflow by truncating context.
acceptanceCriteria: Falls back to primary provider if diagnostic_model not configured
Truncation order: tool_call_log to last 10 entries, raw_output to 1000 chars, previous_attempts to 1-line summaries
Handles JSON parse errors by returning default diagnosis

userStories.title: Create RecoveryStrategy dataclass for strategy ladder output
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a RecoveryStrategy dataclass to structure strategy ladder decisions so that recovery execution has clear instructions. **Implementation:** In recovery/strategy_selector.py, define RecoveryStrategy dataclass matching spec Appendix C.5: strategy_name, tier, strategy_description, constraints, prompt_modifications, scope_reduction, is_terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Define RecoveryStrategy dataclass with all specified fields
strategy_name is one of: fix_specific, rewrite_with_diagnosis, simplify, decompose, escalate
All fields match spec Appendix C.5

userStories.title: Implement select_strategy function for 5-tier strategy ladder
userStories.description: As a developer, I want select_strategy() to choose recovery approach based on attempt number and diagnostic guidance so that recovery escalates appropriately. **Implementation:** In strategy_selector.py, create select_strategy(attempt_number, error_category, diagnostic_output, config) that implements spec Section 4.3 ladder: Tier 1=Fix Specific, Tier 2=Rewrite with Diagnosis, Tier 3=Simplify + Constrain, Tier 4=Decompose Recommendation, Tier 5=Escalate. Returns RecoveryStrategy.
acceptanceCriteria: select_strategy() accepts attempt_number, error_category, diagnostic_output, config
Tier 1 (attempt 1): Returns fix_specific strategy with exact error feedback
Tier 2 (attempt 2): Returns rewrite_with_diagnosis with diagnostic guidance
Tier 3 (attempt 3): Returns simplify with scope reduction and tool constraints
Tier 4 (attempt 4): Returns decompose with recommendations for operator
Special case: errors with max_retries=1 AND requires_diagnosis=True skip Tier 1

userStories.title: Create failure_memory.py module with query functions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want failure_memory.py to query similar past fixes so that recovery learns from history. **Implementation:** Create recovery/failure_memory.py with functions: record_attempt(task_id, error_code, agent_name, diagnostic_summary, resolution_summary, success, recovery_tier, task_domain), find_similar_fixes(error_code, agent_name, task_domain, limit), cleanup_old_entries(retention_days).
acceptanceCriteria: record_attempt() inserts row into failure_memory table
find_similar_fixes() implements 3-tier query: (1) exact match on error_code+agent_name+success=True, (2) match error_code+task_domain, (3) match error_code only
Returns first tier that produces results, limit 3
cleanup_old_entries() deletes rows where created_at older than retention_days

userStories.title: Create RecoveryContext dataclass for enriched retry prompts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a RecoveryContext dataclass to structure recovery prompt injection so that agents receive targeted guidance. **Implementation:** In recovery/recovery_pipeline.py, define RecoveryContext dataclass matching spec Appendix C.2: attempt_number, max_attempts, original_error_code, original_error_message, strategy, constraints, previous_attempts, similar_past_fixes, diagnostic_summary.
acceptanceCriteria: Define RecoveryContext dataclass with all specified fields
similar_past_fixes is list of dicts from failure_memory
All fields match spec Appendix C.2

userStories.title: Create PreviousAttemptSummary dataclass
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a PreviousAttemptSummary dataclass to structure past attempt history in recovery context so that agents see what was tried. **Implementation:** In recovery_pipeline.py, define PreviousAttemptSummary dataclass matching spec Appendix C.4: attempt_number, strategy_used, error_code, error_message, diagnostic_summary, tools_used, duration_ms.
acceptanceCriteria: All fields match spec Appendix C.4
tools_used is list of tool name strings

userStories.title: Implement compensate function for pre-retry cleanup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want compensate() to clean up failed attempt artifacts so that retry starts from known-good state. **Implementation:** Create recovery/compensating_actions.py with compensate(task_id, agent_name, error_code, dispatch_db) that: clears partial working_memory entries (DELETE FROM working_memory WHERE task_id=? AND agent_name=?), resets dispatch_status to 'queued', logs abandoned output.
acceptanceCriteria: compensate() accepts task_id, agent_name, error_code, dispatch_db connection
Deletes working_memory rows for this task+agent only (preserves upstream dependency data)
Resets dispatch_status to 'queued' if error_code requires compensation
Uses atomic UPDATE with WHERE guards

userStories.title: Enhance agent_prompts.py to accept recovery_context parameter
userStories.description: As a developer, I want build_prompt() to inject recovery context into agent prompts so that retry attempts have targeted guidance. **Implementation:** Modify agent_prompts.build_prompt() to accept optional recovery_context: RecoveryContext parameter. If provided, inject recovery section (spec Appendix G format) into USER message after task description, before skills.
acceptanceCriteria: If recovery_context is None, build prompt as usual (no change to existing behavior)
If recovery_context provided, inject <recovery-context> XML block into USER message
Recovery context appears after <task> and before <skills>
Includes attempt number, error, strategy, constraints, previous attempts, similar fixes

userStories.title: Implement recovery_executor.execute function for retry with verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want recovery_executor.execute() to re-run agent with recovery context and verify original error resolved so that recovery validates success. **Implementation:** Create recovery/recovery_executor.py with execute(task_id, recovery_context, config, dispatch_db) that builds enriched prompt, calls agent_runner.run(), verifies result, compares error code against original, returns RunnerResult with success indicator.
acceptanceCriteria: execute() accepts task_id, recovery_context, config, dispatch_db
Compares new error_code (if failed) against original error_code from recovery_context

userStories.title: Create RecoveryOutcome dataclass for pipeline result
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a RecoveryOutcome dataclass to structure the full recovery pipeline result so that supervisor knows final disposition. **Implementation:** In recovery_pipeline.py, define RecoveryOutcome dataclass matching spec Appendix C.9: task_id, success, final_status, attempts_used, winning_strategy, escalation_reason, total_duration_ms, total_cost_usd, error_code, trace_id.
acceptanceCriteria: All fields match spec Appendix C.9

userStories.title: Implement handle_failure function for full recovery pipeline orchestration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want handle_failure() to orchestrate all 8 recovery stages so that task failures trigger structured recovery. **Implementation:** In recovery_pipeline.py, create handle_failure(task_id, runner_error, config, dispatch_db) that runs: (1) CAPTURE via error_capture, (2) CLASSIFY via error_classifier, (3) DIAGNOSE via diagnostic_agent (if required), (4) COMPENSATE via compensating_actions (if required), (5) STRATEGIZE via strategy_selector, (6) EXECUTE via recovery_executor, (7) VERIFY result, (8) LEARN via failure_memory. Returns RecoveryOutcome.
acceptanceCriteria: handle_failure() accepts task_id, runner_error, config, dispatch_db
Stage 1: Calls error_capture.capture_error() and logs recovery.capture event
Stage 2: Calls error_classifier.classify_error() and logs recovery.classify event
Stage 3: Calls diagnostic_agent.diagnose_failure() if error_category.requires_diagnosis=True, logs recovery.diagnose.start/complete events
Stage 4: Calls compensating_actions.compensate() if error_category.requires_compensation=True, logs recovery.compensate event

Stage 5: Calls playbooks.has_playbook(error_code) first; if True uses playbook.select_strategy() to replace generic ladder, otherwise calls strategy_selector.select_strategy(). Logs recovery.strategy.selected event
Stage 6: Calls recovery_executor.execute() with RecoveryContext, logs recovery.execute.start/complete events
Stage 7: Verifies RunnerResult success and compares error codes, logs recovery.verify event
Stage 8: Calls failure_memory.record_attempt() with outcome, logs recovery.learn event
Generates UUID trace_id for correlating all recovery_events
All stages log to recovery_events table

userStories.title: Add atomic failed to recovering status transition
userStories.description: As a developer, I want atomic dispatch_status transition from 'failed' to 'recovering' so that duplicate recovery is prevented. **Implementation:** In recovery_pipeline.py handle_failure(), before starting recovery, execute atomic UPDATE tasks SET dispatch_status='recovering' WHERE id=? AND dispatch_status='failed'. If rowcount=0, return early (another process already claimed recovery).
acceptanceCriteria: handle_failure() begins with atomic UPDATE ... WHERE dispatch_status='failed'
Returns RecoveryOutcome with success=False if rowcount=0 (already recovering)
Logs recovery.claim.failed event if duplicate detected
Logs recovery.claim.success event if transition succeeded

userStories.title: Add recovery budget check before retry
userStories.description: As a developer, I want recovery_executor to check per-task recovery budget before executing retry so that runaway recovery is prevented. **Implementation:** In recovery_executor.execute(), before calling agent_runner.run(), query SUM(cost_estimate) FROM dispatch_runs WHERE task_id=? AND recovery_tier IS NOT NULL. Calculate recovery_budget = min(config.daily_budget_usd * config.recovery.recovery_budget_ratio, config.recovery.recovery_budget_cap_usd). If total_recovery_cost >= recovery_budget, raise RecoveryBudgetExceeded.
acceptanceCriteria: recovery_executor.execute() queries total recovery cost for task
Calculates recovery_budget using config.recovery.recovery_budget_ratio (default 0.04) and recovery_budget_cap_usd (default 2.00)
handle_failure() catches RecoveryBudgetExceeded and escalates immediately

userStories.title: Add max concurrent recoveries guard
userStories.description: As a developer, I want max concurrent recoveries enforcement so that recovery doesn't overwhelm the system. **Implementation:** In recovery_pipeline.py handle_failure(), before starting recovery, query SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tasks WHERE dispatch_status='recovering'. If count >= config.recovery.max_concurrent_recoveries (default 3), defer recovery by setting dispatch_runs.next_retry_at to 60 seconds from now and return early.
acceptanceCriteria: handle_failure() queries count of tasks with dispatch_status='recovering'
If at or over limit, sets next_retry_at to now + 60 seconds
Returns RecoveryOutcome with success=False and escalation_reason='concurrency_limit'
Next poll cycle will retry when slot opens

userStories.title: Add recovery timeout enforcement per attempt
userStories.description: As a developer, I want per-attempt recovery timeout so that stuck recovery doesn't block progress. **Implementation:** In recovery_executor.execute(), wrap agent_runner.run() call with timeout using signal.alarm() or threading.Timer(). If recovery_timeout_per_attempt (default 600s) expires, raise RecoveryTimeoutError. handle_failure() catches this and treats as failed attempt.
acceptanceCriteria: Treats timeout as failed attempt, proceeds to next tier if retries remain

userStories.title: Add total recovery time hard timeout
userStories.description: As a developer, I want max_recovery_time_seconds hard timeout so that recovery pipeline cannot run indefinitely. **Implementation:** In recovery_pipeline.py handle_failure(), track start_time at beginning. Before each retry, check if (now - start_time) > config.recovery.max_recovery_time_seconds (default 1800). If exceeded, escalate immediately regardless of attempts remaining.
acceptanceCriteria: Before each recovery attempt, checks elapsed time
If exceeded, breaks recovery loop and escalates

userStories.title: Integrate recovery pipeline into agent_supervisor handle_dispatch_failure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want agent_supervisor.handle_dispatch_failure() to call recovery_pipeline.handle_failure() so that failures trigger recovery instead of simple retry. **Implementation:** Modify agent_supervisor.handle_dispatch_failure() to call recovery_pipeline.handle_failure() and handle RecoveryOutcome. If final_status='completed', call handle_task_completion(). If final_status='failed', update dispatch_runs.next_retry_at for next poll cycle. If final_status='dispatch_failed', create urgent notification and stop retrying.
acceptanceCriteria: Passes task_id, runner_error, config, dispatch_db to handle_failure()
If final_status='completed': calls handle_task_completion() to flow result to OpenClawd
If final_status='failed': updates next_retry_at for next poll cycle retry
If final_status='dispatch_failed': creates urgent notification with escalation_reason
Preserves existing handle_dispatch_failure behavior as fallback if recovery disabled

userStories.title: Capture error context in agent_runner on all failures
userStories.description: As a developer, I want agent_runner.run() to capture structured error context on all failures so that recovery pipeline has complete information. **Implementation:** Modify agent_runner.run() to: (1) store raw LLM output before validation, (2) accumulate tool_call_log during tool loop, (3) populate RunnerError with full context, (4) write raw_output, tool_call_log, stop_reason, error_context JSON to dispatch_runs on failure.
acceptanceCriteria: agent_runner.run() stores raw LLM response before parsing AgentResult
Tool call loop appends each tool call to tool_call_log list
RunnerError includes: raw_output, tool_call_log, stop_reason fields
On failure, writes to dispatch_runs: raw_output, tool_call_log, stop_reason, error_context JSON
error_context JSON includes exception type, message, traceback
Truncates raw_output to config.recovery.raw_output_max_chars before writing
Does not break existing agent_runner.run() success path

userStories.title: Add recovery configuration section to openclawd.config.yaml
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a recovery section in openclawd.config.yaml so that recovery parameters are configurable. **Implementation:** Add recovery section to openclawd.config.yaml with all parameters from spec Appendix E: max_recovery_attempts, max_recovery_time_seconds, max_concurrent_recoveries, recovery_timeout_per_attempt, diagnostic_model, diagnostic_max_input_tokens, diagnostic_prompt_file, recovery_budget_ratio, recovery_budget_cap_usd, raw_output_max_chars, diagnostic_context_chars, recovery_prompt_context_chars, error_message_max_chars, min_confidence_score, systemic_failure_threshold_count, systemic_failure_window_minutes, max_escalations_per_hour, escalation_cooldown_seconds, failure_memory_retention_days, recovery_events_retention_days.
acceptanceCriteria: All parameters from spec Appendix E included with default values
All truncation and threshold parameters included
Config loader in config.py reads recovery section with fallback to defaults

userStories.title: Add recovery event types to structured_logging
userStories.description: As a developer, I want recovery-specific event types in structured_logging so that recovery pipeline stages are observable. **Implementation:** Add event type definitions to structured_logging.py for: recovery.capture, recovery.classify, recovery.diagnose.start, recovery.diagnose.complete, recovery.compensate, recovery.strategy.selected, recovery.execute.start, recovery.execute.complete, recovery.verify, recovery.learn, recovery.escalate, recovery.claim.success, recovery.claim.failed, recovery.deferred, recovery.timeout, recovery.hard_timeout, recovery.budget.exceeded.
acceptanceCriteria: All recovery event types from spec Section 5.7 defined
Each event includes: timestamp, event_type, task_id, duration_ms (if applicable), metadata dict
Metadata includes: error_code, recovery_tier, strategy_name (as applicable)

userStories.title: Implement failure_memory cleanup task for retention
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automatic failure_memory cleanup so that old entries are purged per retention policy. **Implementation:** In failure_memory.py, implement cleanup_old_entries(retention_days, dispatch_db) that deletes failure_memory rows where created_at older than retention_days. Call from data_retention.py scheduled cleanup sweep.
acceptanceCriteria: cleanup_old_entries() deletes failure_memory rows older than retention_days
DELETE query uses WHERE created_at < date('now', '-{retention_days} days')
Called from data_retention.py sweep (integrate with existing cleanup tasks)

userStories.title: Implement recovery_events cleanup task for retention
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automatic recovery_events cleanup so that observability data doesn't bloat the database. **Implementation:** In recovery_pipeline.py, implement cleanup_recovery_events(retention_days, dispatch_db) that deletes recovery_events rows older than retention_days. Call from data_retention.py scheduled cleanup.
acceptanceCriteria: cleanup_recovery_events() deletes recovery_events rows older than retention_days
DELETE query uses WHERE created_at < date('now', '-{retention_days} days')

userStories.title: Add query_similar_fixes to failure_memory for context enrichment
userStories.description: As a developer, I want query_similar_fixes() to find past successful recoveries so that diagnostic agent and strategy selector learn from history. **Implementation:** In failure_memory.py, implement find_similar_fixes(error_code, agent_name, task_domain, limit) using 3-tier SQL query strategy from spec Section 5.5: (1) exact match on error_code+agent_name+success=True, (2) match error_code+task_domain, (3) match error_code only. Returns list of dicts with resolution_summary.
acceptanceCriteria: find_similar_fixes() accepts error_code, agent_name, task_domain, limit
Tier 1 query: SELECT * FROM failure_memory WHERE error_code=? AND agent_name=? AND success=1 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT ?
If no results, Tier 2: WHERE error_code=? AND task_domain=? AND success=1
If no results, Tier 3: WHERE error_code=? AND success=1
Returns first tier that produces results
Returns list of dicts with: error_code, resolution_summary, diagnostic_summary, recovery_tier

userStories.title: Integrate similar fixes into diagnostic agent input
userStories.description: As a developer, I want diagnostic_agent.diagnose_failure() to include similar past fixes so that diagnosis learns from patterns. **Implementation:** Before calling diagnose_failure(), query failure_memory.find_similar_fixes() and include results in DiagnosticInput.similar_past_fixes field.
acceptanceCriteria: Pass error_code, agent_name, task_domain from ErrorContext
diagnostic_sop.md prompt template includes section for similar_past_fixes
If no similar fixes found, similar_past_fixes is empty list

userStories.title: Add escalation notification for terminal recovery failures
userStories.description: As a developer, I want urgent notifications when recovery escalates so that operators are alerted to persistent failures. **Implementation:** In recovery_pipeline.py handle_failure(), when final_status='dispatch_failed', create notification via notification_delivery with urgency='urgent', include full diagnostic report, recovery history, and escalation_reason.
acceptanceCriteria: Notification content includes: task_id, task_description, error_code, diagnostic_summary, recovery attempts used, escalation_reason

userStories.title: Add Tier 4 decomposition recommendation notification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Tier 4 strategy to create notification with decomposition guidance so that operators can manually split complex tasks. **Implementation:** In strategy_selector.py, when select_strategy() returns Tier 4 (decompose), include decomposition suggestions from diagnostic_output. In handle_failure(), create notification with decomposition recommendations.
acceptanceCriteria: handle_failure() creates notification when strategy is 'decompose'
Content includes: diagnostic_output.specific_fix with decomposition steps
Recommends operator use 'openclawd pipeline create' to split task

userStories.title: Add recovering status to interrupted task recovery sweep
userStories.description: As a developer, I want recover_interrupted_tasks() to reset 'recovering' status on daemon restart so that crashed recoveries are retried. **Implementation:** Modify agent_supervisor.recover_interrupted_tasks() to include dispatch_status='recovering' in query. Reset recovering tasks to 'failed' so they re-enter recovery pipeline on next poll cycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Runs on daemon startup per US-056 spec

userStories.title: Add INCOMPLETE_RESEARCH auto-detection for research tasks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want INCOMPLETE_RESEARCH auto-detection so that research tasks with insufficient sources trigger recovery. **Implementation:** In error_classifier.py detect_error_code(), add post-completion check: if agent_result.status='completed' AND task_domain='research', count distinct URLs (http/https pattern) in deliverable_content. If fewer than 3, classify as INCOMPLETE_RESEARCH.
acceptanceCriteria: If task_domain='research', scans deliverable_content for URL patterns
Regex pattern matches http:// and https:// URLs
Counts distinct URLs (unique full URLs, not just domains)
If count < 3, returns INCOMPLETE_RESEARCH error code
Only applies to completed status (not failed/blocked)

userStories.title: Add STUCK_TASK detection for long-running dispatched tasks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want STUCK_TASK detection so that tasks stuck in 'dispatched' status trigger recovery. **Implementation:** In agent_supervisor.py poll loop, add stuck task detection query: tasks WHERE dispatch_status='dispatched' AND lease_until < now AND (now - dispatch_runs.started_at) > agent_timeout_seconds. Classify as STUCK_TASK and trigger recovery.
acceptanceCriteria: Poll loop queries for stuck tasks: dispatch_status='dispatched' AND lease expired AND duration > agent_timeout_seconds
Alternative detection: lease extended 3+ times with no tool_calls_count increment
On detection, creates RunnerError with STUCK_TASK code
Transitions to 'failed' and triggers recovery pipeline

userStories.title: Coordinate with health/self_healer for provider-level issues
userStories.description: As a developer, I want recovery_pipeline to check provider health before retry so that task recovery doesn't fight provider healing. **Implementation:** In recovery_executor.execute(), before calling agent_runner.run(), query provider_health for the task's assigned provider. If provider is degraded/down, defer recovery by 60 seconds. Log coordination event.
acceptanceCriteria: Checks status for task's assigned provider
If status='degraded' or 'down', defers retry: sets next_retry_at = now + 60s
Allows health/self_healer.py to complete provider healing first

userStories.title: Add systemic failure pattern detection to failure_memory
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pattern detection for systemic failures so that correlated errors trigger system-level alerts. **Implementation:** In failure_memory.py, add detect_systemic_pattern(error_code, window_minutes, threshold_count) that queries failure_memory for same error_code across multiple tasks within time window. If count >= threshold, returns True.
acceptanceCriteria: Queries failure_memory for rows WHERE error_code=? AND created_at > datetime('now', '-{window_minutes} minutes')
Counts distinct task_id for the error_code

userStories.title: Add systemic failure alert on pattern detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want urgent alerts when systemic failure patterns are detected so that operators address root cause. **Implementation:** In recovery_pipeline.py handle_failure(), after recording to failure_memory, call detect_systemic_pattern(). If True, create urgent notification indicating systemic issue affecting multiple tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: If pattern detected, creates urgent notification
Notification content includes: error_code, affected task count, time window
Recommends system-level investigation (check provider health, API keys, database)
Rate-limited to prevent alert storm (max 1 per error_code per hour)

userStories.title: Add error_pattern generation for similarity matching
userStories.description: As a developer, I want error_pattern normalization so that similar errors match even with different specific values. **Implementation:** In error_capture.py capture_error(), generate error_pattern by normalizing error_message: strip task-specific IDs, UUIDs, timestamps, URLs. Store in ErrorContext.error_pattern and failure_memory.error_pattern for future similarity matching.
acceptanceCriteria: Normalization: replace task IDs with <TASK_ID>, UUIDs with <UUID>, timestamps with <TIMESTAMP>, URLs with <URL>
Regex patterns for common ID formats
Future enhancement point: similarity matching can use error_pattern

userStories.title: Add CONFIDENCE_TOO_LOW detection with configurable threshold
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CONFIDENCE_TOO_LOW detection so that agents with low confidence trigger recovery. **Implementation:** In error_classifier.py detect_error_code(), check if agent_result.confidence_score is not None and < config.recovery.min_confidence_score (default 0.3). If true, classify as CONFIDENCE_TOO_LOW.
acceptanceCriteria: If confidence_score is None, skip check (confidence not provided)
Only applies if agent_result.status='completed' (agent completed but not confident)

userStories.title: Add recovery state to openclawd serve GET /status endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want recovery state exposed in GET /status so that operators can monitor active recoveries. **Implementation:** Extend cli.py serve route GET /status to include recovery_state: list of tasks currently in dispatch_status='recovering' with attempt count, current tier, error_code.
acceptanceCriteria: recovery_state is list of dicts with: task_id, attempt_number, recovery_tier, error_code, time_in_recovery_seconds
Joins with dispatch_runs to get recovery metadata
Returns empty list if no active recoveries

userStories.title: Add openclawd logs --recovery filter for recovery event viewing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want openclawd logs --recovery command so that operators can view recovery events without full log noise. **Implementation:** Add --recovery flag to cli.py logs command that filters supervisor.jsonl for event_type starting with 'recovery.'.
acceptanceCriteria: Filters supervisor.jsonl for lines where event_type starts with 'recovery.'
Shows all recovery events: capture, classify, diagnose, compensate, strategize, execute, verify, learn, escalate
Works with --tail flag for live viewing

userStories.title: Add recovery metrics to openclawd doctor diagnostic output
userStories.description: As a developer, I want openclawd doctor to show recovery health metrics so that operators can assess recovery effectiveness. **Implementation:** Extend cli.py doctor command to query recovery statistics: total recoveries attempted (last 24h), success rate by tier, most common error codes, systemic failure patterns, recovery budget utilization.
acceptanceCriteria: openclawd doctor command includes recovery section
Shows: total recovery attempts (24h), success rate overall, success rate by tier (1-5)
Shows: top 5 error codes by frequency
Shows: recovery budget utilization (% of daily budget spent on recovery)

userStories.title: Add recovery cost tracking to dispatch_runs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want recovery cost tracked per attempt so that budget enforcement works. **Implementation:** In recovery_executor.execute(), after agent_runner.run() completes, calculate cost_estimate (tokens_used * provider_cost_per_token) and store in dispatch_runs row with recovery_tier populated.
acceptanceCriteria: Looks up provider cost per token from config
Stores cost_estimate in dispatch_runs.cost_estimate column (use existing column)
Ensures recovery_tier column is populated for recovery attempts
Cost includes both prompt and completion tokens

userStories.title: Add integration test for full recovery pipeline on schema error
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test that simulates RESULT_SCHEMA_INVALID and verifies full recovery pipeline so that end-to-end recovery works. **Implementation:** Create test_recovery_pipeline.py with test that mocks agent_runner to return invalid schema on first attempt, valid result on second attempt. Verify recovery pipeline captures, classifies, diagnoses, strategizes, executes, verifies, learns.
acceptanceCriteria: Test simulates task with RESULT_SCHEMA_INVALID error
Mocks agent_runner.run() to fail first attempt, succeed second
Verifies: ErrorContext captured, error classified as Execution Error, diagnostic agent called, recovery strategy selected, retry executed, success verified, failure_memory recorded
Verifies recovery_events logged for all 8 stages

userStories.title: Create recovery/playbooks.py with deterministic playbook sequences
userStories.description: As a developer, I want error-specific deterministic playbooks so that known error scenarios follow prescribed recovery sequences instead of the generic strategy ladder. **Implementation:** Create agent-dispatch/recovery/playbooks.py with PlaybookStep and Playbook dataclasses (spec Appendix C.6) and playbook definitions for all 7 scenarios from spec Section 7: (1) SEARCH_API_UNAVAILABLE - web search API down with backoff retries, (2) TOOL_CALL_MALFORMED - malformed LLM tool calls with schema examples, (3) RESULT_SCHEMA_INVALID - AgentResult validation failure with format enforcement, (4) TOOL_LOOP_DETECTED - stuck in tool call loop with tool constraints, (5) DEPENDENCY_STALE - task keeps failing despite completed dependencies, (6) SUPERVISOR_CRASH - supervisor daemon crash recovery, (7) SYSTEMIC_FAILURE - multiple tasks fail with same error. Each Playbook replaces ONLY the STRATEGIZE step (step 7c in pipeline) by producing a RecoveryStrategy with pre-defined prompt modifications and constraints. All other pipeline stages (DIAGNOSE, COMPENSATE, EXECUTE, VERIFY, LEARN) still run normally.
acceptanceCriteria: Define PlaybookStep dataclass with fields: stage, action, parameters, condition (matching spec Appendix C.6)
Define Playbook dataclass with fields: error_code, steps, and select_strategy(attempt, error, classification) method
Playbook for SEARCH_API_UNAVAILABLE with backoff retry sequence (30s, 60s, 120s, 300s)
Playbook for TOOL_CALL_MALFORMED with schema example injection
Playbook for RESULT_SCHEMA_INVALID with format enforcement constraints
Playbook for TOOL_LOOP_DETECTED with tool call limit and constraints
Playbook for DEPENDENCY_STALE with dependency re-check and re-dispatch
Playbook for SUPERVISOR_CRASH with state recovery sequence
Playbook for SYSTEMIC_FAILURE with system-level alert and deferral
Playbook.select_strategy() returns RecoveryStrategy with deterministic prompt_modifications and constraints
All 7 playbooks from spec Section 7 implemented
userStories.notes: Must be completed before US-022 (handle_failure) which references playbooks.has_playbook() in the STRATEGIZE step override (spec Section 9.1.1 step 7c). Placed after strategy_selector.py (US-014) since Playbook.select_strategy() returns RecoveryStrategy.

userStories.title: Add openclawd recovery status and recovery history CLI subcommands
userStories.description: As a developer, I want dedicated `openclawd recovery status` and `openclawd recovery history` CLI subcommands so that operators can inspect active recoveries and historical failure patterns. **Implementation:** In cli.py, add `recovery` subcommand group with two commands: (1) `openclawd recovery status` shows all tasks currently in dispatch_status='recovering' with task_id, error_code, recovery_tier, attempt number, and started_at. (2) `openclawd recovery history` queries failure_memory table and shows top 20 error codes by frequency with count, success_rate, and last_seen. Matches spec Section 9.2 US-108.
acceptanceCriteria: openclawd recovery status outputs: task_id, error_code, recovery_tier, attempt, started_at for all tasks with dispatch_status='recovering'
openclawd recovery status returns empty table with message when no active recoveries
openclawd recovery history outputs: error_code, count, success_rate, last_seen for top 20 error codes in failure_memory
openclawd recovery history returns empty table with message when no failure_memory entries
Queries dispatch_runs for recovery metadata in status command
Queries failure_memory table for history command
userStories.notes: Corresponds to spec Section 9.2 US-108. Complements US-046 (/status endpoint) and US-047 (logs --recovery) with dedicated CLI subcommands.
